words.def: in a state like sleep because of an injury or illness, and not able to use your senses
words.example: They found him lying unconscious on the floor.

words.def: to experience something, especially a change or something unpleasant
words.example: to undergo tests/trials/repairs

words.def: under the surface of the ground
words.example: an underground bunker/tunnel

words.def: the knowledge that somebody has about a particular subject or situation
words.example: Students will gain a broad understanding of the workings of Parliament.

words.def: to make yourself responsible for something and start doing it
words.example: to undertake a task/project

words.def: clothes that you wear under other clothes and next to the skin
words.example: I never wear underwear.

words.def: if something is unexpected, it surprises you because you were not expecting it
words.example: Things took an unexpected turn.

words.def: to spread open or flat something that has previously been folded; to become open and flat
words.example: to unfold a map

words.def: having bad luck; caused by bad luck
words.example: He was unfortunate to lose in the final round.

words.def: being the only one of its kind
words.example: Everyone's fingerprints are unique.

words.def: to join together with other people in order to do something as a group
words.example: Nationalist parties united to oppose the government's plans.

words.def: joined together as a political unit or by shared aims
words.example: the United States of America

words.def: the state of being in agreement and working together; the state of being joined together to form one unit
words.example: European unity

words.def: done by or involving all the people in the world or in a particular group
words.example: Such problems are a universal feature of old age.

words.def: the whole of space and everything in it, including the earth, the planets and the stars
words.example: in the universe, Could there be intelligent life elsewhere in the universe?

words.def: not known or identified
words.example: A previously unknown group claimed responsibility for the bombing.

words.def: a report or broadcast that gives the most recent information about something; a new version of something containing the most recent information
words.example: They will send you regular updates by email.

words.def: located above something else, especially something of the same type or the other of a pair
words.example: her upper lip

words.def: towards a higher place or position
words.example: Place your hands on the table with the palms facing upwards.

words.def: connected with a town or city
words.example: urban and rural communities

words.def: to advise or try hard to persuade somebody to do something
words.example: urge somebody to do something, If you ever get the chance to visit this place, I strongly urge you to do so.

words.def: that needs to be dealt with or happen immediately
words.example: The police have issued an urgent appeal for information.

words.def: the way in which words are used in a language
words.example: a book on current English usage

words.def: not useful; not doing or achieving what is needed or wanted
words.example: This pen is useless.

words.def: a person or thing that uses something
words.example: road users

words.def: that is legally or officially acceptable
words.example: Do you have a valid passport?

words.def: how much something is worth in money or other goods for which it can be exchanged
words.example: to go up/rise/increase in value

words.def: a change, especially in the amount or level of something
words.example: The dial records very slight variations in pressure.

words.def: to be different from each other in size, shape, etc.
words.example: New techniques were introduced with varying degrees of success.

words.def: extremely large in area, size, amount, etc.
words.example: a vast area of forest

words.def: a place where people meet for an organized event, for example a concert, sporting event or conference
words.example: The band will be playing at 20 different venues on their UK tour.

words.def: a form of something that is slightly different from an earlier form or from other forms of the same thing
words.example: version of something, the latest version of the software package

words.def: going straight up or down from a level surface or from top to bottom in a picture, etc.
words.example: the vertical axis of the graph

words.def: used to emphasize that you are talking about a particular thing or person and not about another
words.example: He might be phoning her at this very moment.

words.def: through a place
words.example: We flew home via Dubai.

words.def: success in a game, an election, a war, etc.
words.example: to win a narrow victory

words.def: a person watching television or a video on the internet
words.example: The programme attracted millions of viewers.

words.def: a way of thinking about a subject
words.example: from a… viewpoint, Try looking at things from a different viewpoint.

words.def: violent behaviour that is intended to hurt or kill somebody
words.example: Police do not think this killing was a random act of violence.

words.def: made to appear to exist by the use of computer software, for example on the internet
words.example: a system to help programmers create virtual environments

words.def: a stamp or mark put in your passport by officials of a foreign country that gives you permission to enter, pass through or leave their country
words.example: to apply for a visa

words.def: that can be seen
words.example: The house is clearly visible from the beach.

words.def: the ability to see; the area that you can see from a particular position
words.example: to have good/perfect/poor/blurred/normal vision

words.def: of or connected with seeing or sight
words.example: the visual arts

words.def: necessary or essential in order for something to succeed or exist
words.example: vital for somebody/something, the vitamins that are vital for health
